heading.title: Welcome to Maturoscope, the tool that guides your innovation to the market
heading.description: Designed by experts from the building and construction sector. This tool is meant to guide you throughout the entire maturation process of your innovation.

keys.title: TRL - Technology Readiness Level
keys.description: A standardized measurement system, developed by NASA, used to assess the maturity and readiness of a technology.

keys.title: MKRL - Market Readiness Level
keys.description: A scale that evaluates how ready an innovation is for the market, looking at business potential, customer validation, and competitive positioning.

keys.title: MFRL - Manufacturing Readiness Level
keys.description: A scale that assesses the maturity of manufacturing capabilities needed to produce the innovation at scale.

policy.description: METABUILDING LABS Project has received funding from the European Union's Horizon 2020 Research and Innovation Programme under Grant Agreement No. 953193. The sole responsibility for the content of this document lies entirely with the author's view. The European Commission is not responsible for any use that may be made of the information it contains.

heading.title: Why use Maturoscope?
heading.description: Are you developing an innovative solution in the construction sector? Whether it's a product, a process, or a service, Maturoscope allows you to assess its maturity level in just a few clicks and guides you through the next steps towards commercialization.

list.title: Less than 5 minutes
list.description: Complete a quick assessment so you can get back to building faster.

list.title: No account required
list.description: Start instantly—no sign-ups or passwords—so there's zero friction.

list.title: We don't store your information
list.description: Your responses are not stored or shared; all data is deleted when the session ends.

checks.title: Clear understanding of your innovation's maturity
checks.description: Quickly discover where your solution stands in terms of technical, industrial, and market readiness, helping you identify the right next steps.

checks.title: Instant personalized report
checks.description: Receive a detailed summary of your results in a PDF format, making it easy to share and keep track of your progress.

checks.title: Tailored guidance for faster growth
checks.description: Get access to a personalized selection of services designed to accelerate the development and success of your innovation.

stages.name: TRL
stages.title: We've evaluated where your innovation stands technically.
stages.description: Time to see how it fits in the marketplace.

questions.title: Is the technical concept formalized and positioned relative to existing solutions? *

options.title: The concept is only described informally.
A first formalization is available, accompanied by an illustration or diagram.
The solution is described as a whole, with its key functionalities and components. An analysis of existing solutions has been performed (benchmark, state of the art).
All key components are chosen and described, with their functions.
The entire product is described, with components and their interfaces.
The product and its implementation and integration process to the building is described for the target application domain.
A first positive feedback from process implementation has been documented.
Several feedback experiences have been collected and cover the entire target application domain.
Consolidated technical file, ready for commercial or industrial use.

questions.title: Have you conducted feasibility studies and proof-of-concept experiments? *

options.title: No feasibility studies have been conducted yet.
Basic theoretical feasibility has been assessed through literature review.
Initial feasibility studies completed with preliminary positive results.
Comprehensive feasibility analysis conducted covering technical and economic aspects.
Proof-of-concept experiments successfully demonstrate core functionality.
Multiple proof-of-concept tests validate different use cases.
Feasibility confirmed through independent third-party validation.
Comprehensive feasibility documentation supports business case development.
All feasibility concerns addressed with detailed implementation roadmap.

questions.title: What is the current status of your prototype development? *

options.title: No prototype has been developed yet.
Conceptual design and basic sketches are available.
Digital mockups and 3D models have been created.
First physical prototype built with basic functionality.
Working prototype demonstrates core features successfully.
Refined prototype tested under controlled laboratory conditions.
Advanced prototype validated in simulated real-world scenarios.
Multiple prototype iterations tested and optimized.
Production-ready prototype meets all technical specifications.

questions.title: How extensively have you tested your technology's performance? *

options.title: No performance testing has been conducted.
Basic functionality tests completed successfully.
Performance benchmarks established and initial testing begun.
Comprehensive performance testing under various conditions.
Performance consistently meets or exceeds design specifications.
Long-term durability and reliability testing completed.
Performance validated by independent testing laboratories.
Comparative analysis shows superior performance to existing solutions.
All performance metrics documented and certified for commercial use.

questions.title: What level of integration testing have you completed? *

options.title: No integration testing has been performed.
Basic component integration successfully demonstrated.
System integration testing in controlled environment completed.
Integration with existing building systems successfully tested.
Full system integration performs reliably under normal conditions.
Integration testing covers multiple building types and configurations.
System integration validated in real construction environments.
Seamless integration achieved with industry-standard protocols.
Complete integration package ready for commercial deployment.

questions.title: How advanced is your pilot project implementation? *

options.title: No pilot projects have been initiated.
Pilot project planning and site selection in progress.
First pilot project launched with initial implementation.
Pilot project showing promising preliminary results.
Successful pilot project completion with documented outcomes.
Multiple pilot projects across different building types completed.
Pilot projects demonstrate consistent performance and reliability.
Pilot project results support full-scale commercial deployment.
Comprehensive pilot program validates market readiness.

questions.title: What is the status of regulatory compliance and certifications? *

options.title: No regulatory requirements have been identified.
Relevant regulations and standards have been researched.
Compliance strategy developed with regulatory roadmap.
Initial compliance testing and documentation underway.
Major regulatory requirements satisfied with supporting evidence.
Certification applications submitted to relevant authorities.
Key certifications obtained for primary markets.
Full regulatory compliance achieved across target markets.
All certifications current and ready for commercial launch.

stages.name: MKRL
stages.title: We've looked at the technical and market aspects.
stages.description: Now let's assess your production capabilities.

questions.title: Is the technical concept formalized and positioned relative to existing solutions? *

options.title: The concept is only described informally.
A first formalization is available, accompanied by an illustration or diagram.
The solution is described as a whole, with its key functionalities and components. An analysis of existing solutions has been performed (benchmark, state of the art).
All key components are chosen and described, with their functions.
The entire product is described, with components and their interfaces.
The product and its implementation and integration process to the building is described for the target application domain.
A first positive feedback from process implementation has been documented.
Several feedback experiences have been collected and cover the entire target application domain.
Consolidated technical file, ready for commercial or industrial use.

questions.title: How well-defined is your target market and customer segments? *

options.title: Target market has not been identified yet.
General market category identified with basic research.
Primary target segments defined with demographic analysis.
Detailed customer personas developed with specific needs identified.
Market segments validated through surveys and interviews.
Customer acquisition strategy developed for each segment.
Early adopters identified and engaged in development process.
Market segmentation refined based on customer feedback.
Comprehensive market analysis supports go-to-market strategy.

questions.title: What is your current competitive analysis and market positioning? *

options.title: No competitive analysis has been conducted.
Basic awareness of direct competitors in the market.
Comprehensive competitive landscape analysis completed.
Competitive advantages and differentiators clearly identified.
Market positioning strategy developed based on competitive analysis.
Unique value proposition validated against competitor offerings.
Competitive intelligence system established for ongoing monitoring.
Market position strengthened through strategic partnerships.
Dominant market position achieved in target segments.

questions.title: How developed is your pricing strategy and business model? *

options.title: No pricing strategy has been developed.
Basic cost analysis and preliminary pricing estimates available.
Pricing strategy developed based on market research.
Business model defined with multiple revenue streams identified.
Pricing validated through customer interviews and market testing.
Flexible pricing models developed for different customer segments.
Revenue projections and financial models completed.
Pricing strategy optimized based on pilot project results.
Proven business model with established pricing and profitability.

questions.title: What is the status of your marketing and sales strategy? *

options.title: No marketing or sales strategy has been developed.
Basic marketing concepts and sales approach identified.
Marketing strategy developed with key messaging and channels.
Sales process defined with customer acquisition funnel.
Marketing campaigns launched with initial customer engagement.
Sales team trained and active in customer acquisition.
Marketing and sales strategies validated through customer feedback.
Proven marketing ROI and consistent sales performance.
Scalable marketing and sales operations ready for expansion.

questions.title: How established are your customer relationships and partnerships? *

options.title: No customer relationships or partnerships established.
Initial contacts made with potential customers and partners.
Letters of intent or preliminary agreements signed.
Strategic partnerships established with key industry players.
First customers acquired with successful project delivery.
Growing customer base with repeat business and referrals.
Strong partner network supporting market expansion.
Established market presence with loyal customer community.
Market leadership position with extensive partner ecosystem.

questions.title: What is your current market traction and growth trajectory? *

options.title: No market traction or growth metrics available.
Initial market interest and early inquiries received.
Growing market awareness and increasing customer inquiries.
Consistent customer acquisition with positive growth trends.
Strong market traction with accelerating growth rates.
Market leadership emerging with competitive advantages.
Sustainable growth model with predictable revenue streams.
Market expansion into new segments and geographic regions.
Dominant market position with continued expansion opportunities.

stages.name: MfRL
stages.title: Your innovation maturity report is ready.
stages.description: Your innovation has just been evaluated according to three maturity scales. Discover its maturity profile as well as specialized service recommendations.

questions.title: Is the technical concept formalized and positioned relative to existing solutions? *

options.title: The concept is only described informally.
A first formalization is available, accompanied by an illustration or diagram.
The solution is described as a whole, with its key functionalities and components. An analysis of existing solutions has been performed (benchmark, state of the art).
All key components are chosen and described, with their functions.
The entire product is described, with components and their interfaces.
The product and its implementation and integration process to the building is described for the target application domain.
A first positive feedback from process implementation has been documented.
Several feedback experiences have been collected and cover the entire target application domain.
Consolidated technical file, ready for commercial or industrial use.

questions.title: How developed is your manufacturing process design? *

options.title: No manufacturing process has been designed.
Basic manufacturing concepts and requirements identified.
Preliminary manufacturing process flow documented.
Detailed manufacturing process design completed.
Manufacturing process validated through small-scale production.
Process optimization completed with quality controls established.
Manufacturing process scaled and tested at production volumes.
Fully automated manufacturing process with quality assurance.
World-class manufacturing process ready for global production.

questions.title: What is the status of your supply chain and material sourcing? *

options.title: No supply chain analysis has been conducted.
Key materials and components identified with basic sourcing research.
Potential suppliers identified and initial contact established.
Supplier relationships developed with preliminary agreements.
Supply chain established with reliable material sourcing.
Multiple supplier options secured with competitive pricing.
Supply chain optimized for cost, quality, and delivery performance.
Resilient supply chain with risk management and backup suppliers.
Global supply chain network optimized for scalable production.

questions.title: How advanced is your production facility and equipment setup? *

options.title: No production facility or equipment requirements defined.
Basic facility requirements and equipment needs identified.
Production facility plan developed with equipment specifications.
Pilot production facility established with basic equipment.
Production facility operational with necessary equipment installed.
Facility optimized for efficiency with advanced manufacturing equipment.
Full-scale production facility with automated systems operational.
Multiple production facilities with redundancy and scalability.
World-class manufacturing facilities with Industry 4.0 capabilities.

questions.title: What is the status of your quality control and testing systems? *

options.title: No quality control systems have been established.
Basic quality requirements and testing procedures identified.
Quality control plan developed with testing protocols.
Quality testing equipment installed and procedures implemented.
Comprehensive quality assurance system operational.
Quality control system validated and continuously improved.
Advanced quality systems with real-time monitoring and control.
Industry-leading quality standards with third-party certification.
World-class quality systems with zero-defect manufacturing capability.

questions.title: How developed is your workforce and manufacturing expertise? *

options.title: No manufacturing workforce or expertise available.
Basic manufacturing skills and workforce requirements identified.
Key personnel recruited with relevant manufacturing experience.
Manufacturing team trained and operational with basic capabilities.
Skilled manufacturing workforce with established production expertise.
Advanced manufacturing team with continuous improvement capabilities.
Expert manufacturing workforce with industry-leading capabilities.
World-class manufacturing organization with innovation capabilities.
Global manufacturing expertise with knowledge transfer capabilities.

questions.title: What is your current production scalability and cost optimization status? *

options.title: No scalability analysis or cost optimization conducted.
Basic production cost analysis and scalability assessment completed.
Scalability plan developed with cost optimization strategies.
Production scaling demonstrated with improved cost efficiency.
Scalable production achieved with competitive cost structure.
Production optimization delivering industry-competitive costs.
Highly scalable production with cost leadership in target markets.
Global production scalability with optimized cost structure.
World-class production efficiency with lowest-cost position in industry.
